title: Structure and tectonics of the erosional convergent margin off Antofagasta, north Chile (23°30′S)
journal: Journal of Geophysical Research: Solid Earth
abstract: Coincident wide-angle seismic refraction and near-vertical seismic profiling, combined with gravity data, were used to study the Antofagasta sector (23°30′S) of the north Chilean convergent continental margin where subduction erosion has dominated the evolution since at least the Mesozoic. A 2-D velocity field of the overriding and subducting plates was obtained using joint refraction and reflection travel time tomography. Gravity and velocity models indicate the overriding plate is mainly composed of arc-type igneous basement. The study is based on data from the CINCA (Convergent Subduction off North Chile and Antofagasta) experiment.

location.description: Offshore Antofagasta at 23°30′S, northern Chilean convergent margin, CINCA experiment

seismic_lines.name: SO104-401 — CINCA95 wide-angle refraction profile at 23°30′S; 225 km long, 15 OBH + 4 PDAS land stations

data.name: CINCA Wide-angle OBS seismic refraction data (23°30′S)
data.description: Wide-angle seismic refraction data from the CINCA experiment (1995) at 23°30′S offshore Antofagasta. OBS instruments recorded airgun shots for joint refraction-reflection travel time tomography.

data.name: CINCA Near-vertical MCS reflection data (23°30′S)
data.description: Near-vertical incidence MCS reflection profiles from CINCA experiment at 23°30′S offshore Antofagasta, coincident with wide-angle profile.

data.name: Multibeam bathymetry from SONNE cruises SO104 and SO244 — north Chile

processing.summary: Coincident wide-angle seismic refraction and near-vertical MCS reflection data from the CINCA experiment (1995) at 23°30′S offshore Antofagasta processed to produce a 2D velocity model. Joint refraction and reflection travel time tomography used to obtain the velocity field. Gravity forward modelling applied as additional constraint. The resulting models reveal arc-type igneous basement of the overriding plate and document long-term subduction erosion.
workflow: 1. OBS (15 OBH IFM-GEOMAR) and land station (4 PDAS GFZ-Potsdam) deployment along SO104-401 profile at 23°30′S
2. Airgun shooting: 3 × 30-L air guns (5492 cui total) fired every 60 s (150 m spacing) from R/V SONNE
3. Wide-angle travel time picking: Pg, Pn, PiP phases; picking errors 50 ms (near offset), 60 ms (far offset), 80 ms (PiP); 3048 first arrivals + 721 PiP picked
4. 1-D velocity models: best-fit models for oceanic plate (3 OBS) and continental margin (lower slope OBHs 4–8, upper slope OBHs 9–15)
5. 2-D reference model construction by linear interpolation of 1-D models; initial PiP geometry from MCS PSDM section (von Huene & Ranero 2003) + Antofagasta earthquake aftershocks
6. Joint refraction-reflection travel time tomography (Korenaga et al. 2000): 10 iterations; 2-D velocity field + floating reflector (interplate boundary); horizontal grid 0.5 km, vertical 0.1–1 km
7. Monte Carlo uncertainty analysis for oceanic plate velocity parameters
8. Checkerboard and spike tests for resolution assessment
9. 2-D gravity forward modelling using velocity-derived density (Birch 1961; Christensen & Mooney 1995)
10. Porosity derivation from velocity using Raymer-Hunt-Gardner (1980) transform
11. Integration with PSDM MCS section (von Huene & Ranero 2003) and multibeam bathymetry for tectonic interpretation
software: Korenaga et al. (2000) joint refraction-reflection travel time tomography code (TOMO2D)
processing.notes: CINCA95 cruise: SO-104/1, 22 July–24 August 1995 (Hinz et al. 1995 cruise report). 3 × 30-L air guns (5492 cui), 60 s / 150 m shot interval. 15 OBH (IFM-GEOMAR) + 4 PDAS land stations (GFZ-Potsdam / Free Univ. Berlin). Land records provided by Stefan Lueth (FU Berlin). Profile 225 km long. MCS near-vertical data from von Huene & Ranero (2003), not newly acquired here — pre-stack depth migrated. Funded by German BMBF.

analysis_notes: Validated against PDF. Title, authors (2), DOI confirmed. vessel: 'R/V SONNE' → 'R/V SONNE (SO104, CINCA95, July–August 1995)' (confirmed in acknowledgments: 'CINCA data collected during R/V SONNE cruise 104'). source_volume_cui: null → 5492 (3 × 30-L air guns = 90L × 61.02). channel_count: null → 19 (15 OBH IFM-GEOMAR + 4 PDAS GFZ-Potsdam/FU Berlin). seismic_lines[0] updated to SO104-401, length_km=225. data[1].data_type: 'seismic_refraction_obs' → 'seismic_reflection_mcs' (near-vertical MCS data). workflow expanded to 11 steps based on methods section; software added: Korenaga et al. (2000) TOMO2D. MCS section from von Huene & Ranero (2003), not newly acquired in this study. Data not in public repository.
